Book of the Rings  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WIS: +8 HP: +20
SV FIRE: +10 SV COLD: +10
WT: 2.0 Size: TINY
Class: DRU SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

This item drops off a goblin fanatic in the FM goblin caves, the fanatic can spawn in the slave room.

▲ Page top
better than tov
QuoteReply
# Oct 14 2002 at 11:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779080153
34 posts
for a lower lvl character, tov is better to have, but as you get higher, it loses a lot of the value it had. 10 mana is really nothing when you are lvl 30+, each spell costs about 150+ mana. the saves on this make it much better to have, and the effect on this becomes useless after lvl 22. 2 wis lower is not much to lose, when u get 10 sv fire and sv cold oh, and i just bought it for 375 pp, lol, price is going way down with the bazaar up

Edited, Mon Oct 14 12:21:27 2002
▲ Page top
Vs ToV
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2002 at 11:00 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592757593666
22 posts
compare:
crystalline eye: 30hp, mana; 10svf, svm, svc
ToV: 10wis 10mana mana preservation 1
Book of the rings: 8wis; 10svc, svf, 20hp

now at lvl 40 with less than 200wis that 8wis becomes more than 30 mana, not too much difference between 20hp and 30hp at that lvl either. so only advantage c.e. has is 10svm
as for ToV, the mana is slighly more (by around oh 30 or so), but there is zilch for anything else. which means both eye and book have 10svf svc on it.

lvl 40 druids typically will run into a caster mob every once in a while at a minimum (i do quite often). svm helps a TON, but as most mobs (and pc's) nukes are often fire or cold based, a high svf or svc is extremely important. MR can be made up on any number of items (as can FR and CR) but for the combination of stats, the book has it hands down. more mana than c.e. with comparable stats, and better overall stats than Tov with slighly less mana.

so in keeping my wisdom 190ish i at the same time up my resists. seems like an obvious choice to me but then again i may be wrong. (oh btw at lvl 40, you definately can xp in droga with a good group and clean house just so you don't say "but the price of this item is too high")..

a lvl 40 druid who knows how to play dungeons can quickly become adept to playing in droga. not too many wondering mobs, snare and root stick quite well, and almost nothing (except perhaps the highest lvl mobs in there) sees invis. oh and shroud of nature drops in there =)

--Athenya Battledruid of the 47th circle
--Dedlysilent Suicidal monk of the 45th ring
--Missgiggly "please dont' kill me" 33rd lvl chanter

--Vallon Zek, where it's all about honor, glory, and peekay!
▲ Page top

Tov is mana preservation I

RE: ToV
QuoteReply
# Jun 08 2002 at 5:13 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591764012611
30 posts
I disagree, this item is much better than the ToV because the ToV's effect works only on spells below level 20. If you're a level 24+ druid this might be a better choice then the ToV, mind you, you could keep it for when you're taking out lower mobs with lower spells.

Vs testament of Vanear
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2002 at 3:09 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757499759
View User Forum
133 posts
I've done vanear quest, and what happens when you out grow it and need something better? <waves> bye bye - time and effort, vs no material gain, since vanear is no drop this item is way better, when you out grow it you can sell it, and get 2k+ know what I'd do if i had my time again.)

Slacethehard

lvl52 shaman
▲ Page top
RE: Vs testament of Vanear
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2002 at 4:42 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591771214068
1 post
"when you out grow it"....problem with that is that it's not going to be workth 2k unless you're going to outgrow it within the next month or two. With all of the new items coming out, items are losing their value quickly, on my server at least. This is a nice book and would be one I switch out as need arises, but I don't think it's a permanent replacement for the ToV.

Gm Event
QuoteReply
# Sep 23 2001 at 11:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760089689
8 posts
This book was given as a reward today on Drinal server. I spoke to the GM and assembled a group of 35-40's in LOIO, and we went to the swamp of no hope. We killed something called Lozz Nasae, looked like a giant black sperm cell with teeth, got it's head and gave it to a GM named Hierophant Sakar. Got this and a Spiked goblin Ring.
▲ Page top
RE: Gm Event
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2001 at 11:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760813513
8 posts
same thing happened on xegony that day. a young iksar who gathered the group got the book and we randomed the ring to the 3 under lvl 30 iksars left. though having a 54 monk and 45 monk made the quest far too easy =p the gm should have told me and the 45 monk to get lost LOL.
▲ Page top
This vs. ToV
QuoteReply
# Sep 17 2001 at 11:05 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
If you loot this book yourself, or receive from a friend, it is certainly a nice item. However, before you pay 2k+ for it please note that the Testament of Vanear quest is really easy for anyone level 20 or up. The saves on this are nice, but I'd rather have the extra 2 wis and 10 mana on the ToV. Especially since it only cost me 2pp (to get the book and other page from modooro).

If you decide to get the ToV, don't camp it. just camp your character in highhold pass, and log on early morning or midday. I have checked to see if he was up several times, and he has always been ther (on rodcet nife). Never there at night and usually someone camping his spawn point.
▲ Page top
Book of Rings vs. Testament of Vaneer
QuoteReply
# Oct 15 2001 at 3:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755359730
Scholar
60 posts
This book is way better at high level than the Testament. Once you already have that 200 wis the saves stats will be much more important to you.
▲ Page top
RE: Book of Rings vs. Testament of Vaneer
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2001 at 5:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Yes, but if you are high level enough that your wis is maxxed, and you need resists, and can afford this, buy a crystalline eye instead because it also has +10 save magic, is 1 stone lighter, and has 30mana, which is better if wis is maxed. If, on the other hand, you are still trying to max your wisdom, the testament is better, and can be quested at level 15 or so, and only costs a couple platinum to do the quest. My original point stands, most people looking at this item, are after the wis boost. for that, this is very expensive, and inferior to Testament of Vanear. For resists, if you can't find the crystalline eye, and need resist gear right away, this would be an ok item.
▲ Page top
RE: Book of Rings vs. Testament of Vaneer
QuoteReply
# Mar 06 2002 at 11:00 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767777428
41 posts
It's not about being able to buy something it's that ToV (which by the way I have) is no drop and you cant sell it when you want to upgrade.

Scholar
161 posts
so many primary items with wis no prob, with current day prices in bazaar for a druid a dark ember is easily obtained. And has a nice 14hp proc if you choose to melee. Or if you get really rich try to buy a thundering mace and besides healing your friends occasionally and assist in the melee. Ppl forget that druids are 3rd line melees too often. (wich means engaging after true tanks and meleeclasses)

RE: Secondary slot?
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 3:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764637577
Scholar
18 posts
You want a good item for primary slot, find yourself a Spirit Tome for about 1k. This is a great item, I believe it's +6 wis/int, and can be used in primary or secondary. I have this in my secondary with Paw of Opolla in the other.
▲ Page top
Happy!
QuoteReply
# Jul 25 2001 at 9:44 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592249576088
37 posts
I'm so happy, I just had to post! Was in Lake of Ill Omen today when a GM, Heirophant Sakar, said he had a story to tell. I went to listen, thinking maybe it'd be something interesting and he told a tale of dispair. His brother was killed by a beast by the name of Lozz Nasae. Before the old Heirophant died, he wished to see the head of the beast that sley his brother. So, he sent a group of adventures into the swamp. Luckily, I got on the group and we raced into the Swamp of No Hope. I tracked the little bugger down, and after some trouble with other people be around, we took down the MoB. We take the head of the beast back to LoIO and give it to Sakar, and he gives us the rewards. A Goblin Spiked Ring and a Book of the Rings. Being the only druid in the group, and no shaman in there, I got the book! Woot! Anywho, the saves beat out the Testament of Vaneair, obviously. So, I'm very happy! Great book!

Quail
24 Druid of Karana
Saryrn Server
▲ Page top
RE: Happy!
QuoteReply
# Aug 06 2001 at 8:56 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
IF it's saves you need, I guess this is better, but the ToV gives 10wis and 10mana, which is better than this. Plus, if you happen to play on a PVP server (I don't) the No Drop of ToV is better also. I'd say both are excellent items, and which is better really depends on your needs.
▲ Page top
RE: Happy!
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2001 at 4:31 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763394427
3 posts
Only one pvp server that has item loot at all, and you can't loot held items on that one anyway.. I use this on my pvp shammy when i'm caster fighting, switching it out with my cgs for general levelling and melee's, nice item.
▲ Page top
Goblin Fanatic
QuoteReply
# May 14 2001 at 11:16 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592246538759
8 posts
I attended a guild raid of Droga this weekend. This book was dropped, and the fanatic popped about 6-7 times while we were there. He seems to be a common spawn, but no one specific spot. From what I saw, he can spawn in any of the spawn locations in the pool room and immediate surrounding area. Only one Book of the Rings dropped, and two of the Iksar necro books. Hope this helps
